title: cyanea mauiensis
intro: Cyanea mauiensis, also known as the Maui hawthorn or Ma'o, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the island of Maui in Hawaii. It is a shrub-like plant with small white flowers and red berries, traditionally used by the Native Hawaiians for its medicinal and spiritual properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The leaves and roots of the Cyanea mauiensis plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for various herbal preparations.
